---
title: "Reviewing Captured Pages"
---

You can review any page of the captured batch documents in the image viewer on the left side of the document review layout. Reviewing a page may include adjusting its display size or position to allow you to more effectively assess the page's image quality and content.

To review captured pages in a batch:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If there are no pending batches, capture documents to create a new batch (see Capturing Documents). The document review layout is displayed.  Note:
If documents in the batch are still being captured, the focus is automatically set to the most recently captured page unless you change the focus manually.

3.  In the thumbnail viewer on the right side of the layout, set the focus to the page you want to review by clicking the page or using the arrow keys (see Organizing Captured Documents for more information). The page is displayed in the image viewer on the left side of the layout, as shown in the following example:   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
4.  To change the way the page is scaled in the image viewer, select one of the following fit modes:

| Fit Mode | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Fit Content | Fit Content displays the entire page of a document in the viewer. |
| Fit Height | Fit Height displays the pages of the document at full height. |
| Fit Width | Fit Width displays the pages of the document at full width. |
| Actual Size | Actual Size displays the page at its actual size. |
| Manual | Manual is automatically selected when the zoom level of the page is changed. |

5.  To change the zoom level of the page, do one of the following:
-
To zoom in or out on the page, select the + or - icons. You can also press the + or - keys.

-
To scale the page to a certain percentage of the original document, enter a percent value in the field. In the following example, the page will be scaled to 18% of its original size:

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
6.  To rotate the page, select one of the following options:

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

| Rotation Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Rotate Left | Rotates the page 90 degrees counter-clockwise. You can also press Ctrl + Left. |
| Rotate 180 | Rotates the page 180 degrees. You can also press Ctrl + Down. |
| Rotate Right | Rotates the page 90 degrees clockwise. You can also press Ctrl + Right. |

7.  To display the page in a fullscreen view, select the Fullscreen icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
To exit fullscreen view, press Esc or select the Exit Fullscreen icon.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
8.  To pan across a page, click and hold the mouse pointer and then drag the mouse across the page.
9.  Review additional pages in the batch as needed. For information on organizing pages and documents in the batch, see Organizing Captured Documents.
